meta.title: Radio input
meta.description: A set of closely related, mutually exclusive or complementary actions that are important enough to be displayed directly in the interface for quick access.

mdx:
## Overview

Radio inputs ensure users can easily select one option from a small, visible set
of mutually exclusive choices with clear labels and visual feedback.

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/gHbAJGfcrCv7f2bgzUQgHq/NIMBUS-Guidelines?node-id=1852-4333&m=dev)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Visual options

#### Default

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <RadioInput.Root orientation="horizontal" name="default-example">
    <RadioInput.Option value="option1">Select me</RadioInput.Option>
    <RadioInput.Option value="option2">Select me instead</RadioInput.Option>
  </RadioInput.Root>
)
```

#### Active

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <RadioInput.Root orientation="horizontal" name="active-example" defaultValue="option1">
    <RadioInput.Option value="option1">Selected option</RadioInput.Option>
    <RadioInput.Option value="option2">Unselected option</RadioInput.Option>
  </RadioInput.Root>
)
```

#### Disabled

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <RadioInput.Root orientation="horizontal" name="disabled-example" isDisabled>
    <RadioInput.Option value="option1">Disabled option</RadioInput.Option>
    <RadioInput.Option value="option2">Also disabled</RadioInput.Option>
  </RadioInput.Root>
)
```

#### Invalid toned

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <RadioInput.Root orientation="horizontal" name="invalid-example" isInvalid>
    <RadioInput.Option value="option1">Invalid option</RadioInput.Option>
    <RadioInput.Option value="option2">Another invalid option</RadioInput.Option>
  </RadioInput.Root>
)
```

overview.mdx:
## Overview

Radio inputs ensure users can easily select one option from a small, visible set
of mutually exclusive choices with clear labels and visual feedback.

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/gHbAJGfcrCv7f2bgzUQgHq/NIMBUS-Guidelines?node-id=1852-4333&m=dev)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Visual options

#### Default

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <RadioInput.Root orientation="horizontal" name="default-example">
    <RadioInput.Option value="option1">Select me</RadioInput.Option>
    <RadioInput.Option value="option2">Select me instead</RadioInput.Option>
  </RadioInput.Root>
)
```

#### Active

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <RadioInput.Root orientation="horizontal" name="active-example" defaultValue="option1">
    <RadioInput.Option value="option1">Selected option</RadioInput.Option>
    <RadioInput.Option value="option2">Unselected option</RadioInput.Option>
  </RadioInput.Root>
)
```

#### Disabled

```jsx live
const App = () => (
```

#### Invalid toned

```jsx live
const App = () => (
```

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <RadioInput.Root
    orientation="horizontal"
    name="accessibility-example"
    id="accessibility-example-group"
    aria-label="Select an option"
    isRequired={true}
  >
    <RadioInput.Option
      value="option1"
      id="option1"
      aria-label="Select me option"
    >
      Select me
    </RadioInput.Option>
    <RadioInput.Option
      value="option2"
      id="option2"
      aria-label="Select me instead option"
    >
      Select me instead
    </RadioInput.Option>
  </RadioInput.Root>
)
```

### Accessibility standards

- Always provide a visible label for the radio input.
- Clearly associate the label with the input using the `for` attribute on the
  `<label>` and a matching `id` on the `<input type="radio">`.
- Group related radio inputs within a `<fieldset>` with a `<legend>` to provide
  a clear group label for screen readers.
- Ensure sufficient color contrast for all elements (text, borders, icons, focus
  indicators).
- Clearly indicate the focused state.
- Support keyboard navigation:
- Use Tab to navigate into and out of the radio group.
- Use arrow keys (left/right or up/down) to navigate between radio inputs within
  the group.
- Selected state should be clearly indicated.
- Communicate errors clearly, using both visual cues (e.g., color) and
  descriptive error messages.

## Resources

- [Adobe Spectrum Design System](https://spectrum.adobe.com/)
- [React Spectrum](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/)
- [W3C ARIA Authoring Practices Guide (APG)](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/)
  (Search for "Radio Button" pattern)

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

Radio input guidelines focus on providing a clear, accessible, and mutually
exclusive set of options, ensuring users can easily understand the available
choices and select only one, with clear visual indication of the selected state
and proper labeling for accessibility.

### Best practices

- **Clear and concise labels:** Ensure each radio input has a descriptive label
  placed consistently (usually to the right).
- **Logical grouping:** Enclose related radio inputs within a `<fieldset>`
  element and provide a clear `<legend>` to describe the group's purpose.
- **Visual distinction:** Clearly indicate the selected state (e.g., filled
  circle) and the non-selected state (e.g., empty circle).
- **Sufficient spacing:** Provide enough spacing between radio inputs and their
  labels for easy selection.
- **Keyboard accessibility:** Ensure users can navigate through radio inputs
  using the Tab key and select an option using the arrow keys and Spacebar.
- **Clear default selection (use sparingly):** Only pre-select a radio input if
  there's a genuinely logical default option. Avoid forcing a choice
  unnecessarily.
- **Avoid too many options:** If you have a long list of mutually exclusive
  choices, consider a dropdown select instead.

### Usage

Radio inputs are used when you need users to pick a single choice from a limited
number of options, presenting all possibilities upfront for easy comparison and
selection of just one.

> [!TIP]\
> When to use

- **Single selection required:** When users must choose only one option from a
  predefined list.
- **Limited number of options:** When the number of choices is relatively small
  (typically 2-5) and all options can be displayed clearly.
- **Equal importance of options:** When all choices are presented as equally
  valid and the user needs to make a deliberate selection.
- **Visual comparison is beneficial:** When seeing all available options at once
  helps users understand and compare them before choosing.
- **Clear and mutually exclusive choices:** When the options are distinct and
  there's no possibility of selecting more than one.

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use

- **Multiple selections allowed:** If users can select more than one option, use
  checkboxes instead.
- **Large number of options:** For long lists of choices, a dropdown select is
  more space-efficient.
- **Obscure or infrequently used options:** If many options are rarely chosen, a
  dropdown can hide them until needed, reducing visual clutter.
- **Saving screen space is critical:** Radio inputs display all options, which
  can take up significant space, especially on smaller screens.
- **Default selection is strongly preferred:** If one option is almost always
  the correct choice, a pre-selected checkbox might be more efficient (with
  clear indication that it can be unchecked).
- **Complex or dynamic options:** When the available choices change frequently
  or depend on other user inputs, a dynamic select or autocomplete field might
  be better.

### Limited options

Radio inputs work best when there are a limited number of options for a user.

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - Use when there are a few options available for the user.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <FormField.Root>
    <FormField.Label>Shipping priority</FormField.Label>
    <FormField.Input mt="200">
      <RadioInput.Root orientation="vertical" name="shipping-speed">
        <RadioInput.Option value="standard">Standard</RadioInput.Option>
        <RadioInput.Option value="express">Express</RadioInput.Option>
        <RadioInput.Option value="priority">Priority</RadioInput.Option>
        <RadioInput.Option value="same-day">Same-day</RadioInput.Option>
        <RadioInput.Option value="instore-pickup">
          In-store pick up
        </RadioInput.Option>
      </RadioInput.Root>
    </FormField.Input>
  </FormField.Root>
);
```

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Do not use radio inputs when there are an excessive amount of options. Radio
>   input options on default will show every option to a user so it can be
>   overwhelming for a user when there is a lot of information to digest in a
>   small area. Instead, use a dropdown selector to simplify the UI and make it
>   easier to search quickly to find the right option.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <FormField.Root>
    <FormField.Label>Shipping priority</FormField.Label>
    <FormField.Input mt="200">
      <RadioInput.Root name="shipping-dont">
        <Grid
          templateColumns="repeat(3, auto)"
          gap="400"
          rowGap="400"
          justifyItems="start"
        >
          <RadioInput.Option value="standard">Standard</RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="next-business">
            Next business day
          </RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="local-pickup">
            Local pickup
          </RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="express">Express</RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="express-2">Express</RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="rural">
            Rural/remote area shipping
          </RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="priority">Priority</RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="two-day">
            Two-day shipping
          </RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="temp-controlled">
            Temperature-controlled shipping
          </RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="sameday">Same-day</RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="freight">
            Freight shipping
          </RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="white-glove">
            White glove delivery
          </RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="pickup">In-store pick up</RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="intl-economy">
            International economy
          </RadioInput.Option>
          <RadioInput.Option value="carbon-neutral">
            Carbon-neutral shipping
          </RadioInput.Option>
        </Grid>
      </RadioInput.Root>
    </FormField.Input>
  </FormField.Root>
);
```
